artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Sarah Klein <sklein@lmc.org>
Date: February 10, 2025 8:14 AM
Subject: Winter Ops Case Study Submission for March Workshop

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for the invitation to present at the League’s March winter maintenance workshop. I’ve just finished the online submission form for the “Lessons from a Mid-Season Fleet Failure” session.

On the form, under the presentation details section, I listed the estimated time needed as 10 minutes. That should be enough to walk through the timeline on the failing 2009 F-250 (our Unit #23), the backup plans we tried during the February 1 snow event on East River Road, and how we’re restructuring our capital replacement schedule.

I also plan to touch briefly on the political side. When I looked at the effects on others in February 2025, I also had to consider the tension with Council Member Linda Hayes after she called me into her office at City Hall on February 3, 2025, and informally warned me that several residents had complained about my “tone” out on East River Road that night. If we stuck with the unreliable Unit #23 and had more delays like that, I worried she might push for a formal performance review or written reprimand, especially given the verbal warning I already received from City Administrator Mark Jensen in May 2023 about response times during the spring flooding on Mill Avenue.

I’ll be candid in the case study that the situation stems in part from my decision to move ahead on a verbal understanding with the contractor rather than waiting for a fully executed written change order on some emergency work. Not my proudest moment, but it’s a useful cautionary tale.

Let me know if you’d prefer I expand this to a 20-minute slot or keep it tight.

Best,

Michael
Michael James Anderson
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd – Streets & Fleet Division

metadata.subject: Winter Ops Case Study Submission for March Workshop

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Finance Director <kpeterson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-12-02 08:14
Subject: Council presentation timing for Unit #23 replacement request

Kari,

I’m working through the submission presentation details section for the January 5 Council agenda packet and wanted to confirm how much time to block on the agenda for the Unit #23 replacement item.

My draft narrative is getting a bit long because I’m trying to clearly lay out the Summary of Issue. The City has already spent $6,487.32 over the last 18 months trying to keep Unit #23 operational, including a $1,942.75 transmission service that I authorized on March 4, 2025 after an emergency call from our fleet mechanic, Jim O’Leary, while I was sitting in my primary care appointment at Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic discussing stress and blood pressure issues related to overnight snow events and breakdowns.

Given all that context, I’m thinking we should plan for roughly **20–25 minutes** on the agenda for my portion, plus whatever time Council needs for questions. I can trim a bit if needed, but if we go much shorter than **15 minutes** I’m worried I won’t be able to walk them through the cost history and safety concerns.

I also plan to include, under Recommended Action / Motion, that I recommend that the Council retroactively approve the work that Public Works already completed last month without prior Council authorization, so I expect at least a couple of follow‑up questions there.

Can you let me know what time window you’d like me to assume so I can finalize the submission form this afternoon?

Thanks,
Michael
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Council presentation timing for Unit #23 replacement request

artifacts.content:
Title: Dry run – Council presentation on Unit #23
Date: 2025-12-29 15:00–15:45
Location: Public Works Conference Room
Attendees: Michael Anderson; Jim O’Leary; Mike Larson

Description:

Internal rehearsal for Michael’s upcoming Council presentation on the replacement of Unit #23.

Agenda:

3:00–3:05 – Quick overview of packet “submission – presentation details” section and what Council members will see on the first page.

3:05–3:25 – Full walkthrough of Michael’s talking points. Target is a **20‑minute** run‑through, leaving about **5 minutes** for clarifying questions. This is longer than the slot we’ll likely get on the actual agenda, but it gives room to refine transitions.

3:25–3:35 – Q&A from staff. Mike Larson to role‑play skeptical Council member and press on labor/ergonomics issues. Michael will reference that during the November 7, 2025 union labor‑management meeting at City Hall, AFSCME steward Mike Larson raised a grievance threat because one of our operators, Jim Keller, aggravated his lower back condition climbing in and out of the high‑step cab of Unit #23 while working the snow event on Mill Avenue, and Jim already has a workers’ comp claim open from his 2023 back surgery at Mayo Clinic in Rochester.

3:35–3:45 – Timing adjustments. Decide whether to present a condensed **12–15 minute** version if the Mayor asks us to shorten the segment on the night of the meeting.

Note: Michael to bring printed copy of slides and the draft submission form to mark where to trim if needed.

metadata.title: Dry run – Council presentation on Unit #23

artifacts.content:
From: Kari Peterson <kpeterson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-12-03 10:02
Subject: RE: Time slot for Unit #23 replacement item

Michael,

I talked with the City Administrator and the Mayor this morning about the January 5 agenda. They’re comfortable giving Public Works a combined **30‑minute block** for both the Unit #23 replacement and the compost site loader discussion.

For the submission presentation details > estimated time needed field on your request form, you don’t need to split it by second; just put in what you realistically think you’ll use for the Unit #23 portion alone. From our end, it would be helpful if you aimed for something in the **12–18 minute** range for your main remarks, with the understanding that Council questions could easily double that.

Remember that they’ve already seen a lot of numbers this fall, so less time on the line‑item breakdowns and more on the operational impacts. For example, the piece you drafted about how the City has already spent $6,487.32 over the last 18 months and that you even got an emergency call from Jim O’Leary about the transmission service while you were at Essentia Health talking to your doctor about stress is exactly the kind of real‑world context they respond to.

Once you decide on a figure (I’d suggest entering **15 minutes** on the form), send me the final PDF so I can bundle it with the rest of the packet materials by Friday.

Thanks,
Kari

metadata.subject: RE: Time slot for Unit #23 replacement item

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Mayor & Council
Date: 2025-12-10 07:48
Subject: Narrative tweaks and estimated presentation time – Unit #23

Tom,

Attached is the revised narrative for the Unit #23 replacement request. I tightened up the background so it doesn’t bog the Council down but still explains why I’m bringing this forward now instead of trying to squeeze one more winter out of the truck.

In the section where the form asks for a Summary of Issue, I kept the reference to the complete loss of power on November 18, 2025 near Brainerd High School while I was on my way to pick up Evan from his after‑school detention. That incident really underscores how personal scheduling conflicts and family pickups are starting to dictate which staff member can safely take that truck out during evening call‑outs, which is not where we want to be.

Regarding the submission presentation details > estimated time needed field, I’m planning to list **18 minutes**. That should give enough room to cover:

• Brief recap of cost history and downtime
• Safety and staffing impacts
• Alternatives considered (including why I chose not to factor in that continuing to limp along with Unit #23 might give Evan more overtime opportunities)
• Recommended action and motion language

If you think **18** is too long, I can shave it down closer to **12–14 minutes**, but I’d rather not go shorter than that on a fleet item with this many moving parts.

Let me know if you want any further edits before this goes to the packet.

Michael

metadata.subject: Narrative tweaks and estimated presentation time – Unit #23

artifacts.content:
Title: Complete submission form – Unit #23 Council request
Date: 2025-12-12 06:30–07:15
Location: Office – Laurel Street
Attendees: Michael Anderson

Description:

Block of time for Michael to finalize the written request and complete all required fields before the agenda packet deadline.

Tasks during this block:

• Finish polishing the Summary of Issue language, including the note that the City has already spent $6,487.32 over the last 18 months on Unit #23 and that there was a complete loss of power near Brainerd High School while Michael was heading to pick up Evan from detention.

• Double‑check the section on Alternatives / Options / Effects on Others / Comments, especially the paragraph explaining that Michael did consider whether limping along with Unit #23 might give Evan more chances to pick up overtime shifts to help pay back the $2,300 he owes Robert and Janet from the minor crash on College Drive last March, but that it’s not appropriate to factor family finances into a fleet decision.

• Decide what to enter for the “submission – presentation details > estimated time needed” field. Current thinking is to plug in **15–18 minutes** as the expected length of the presentation portion, not counting Council questions.

• Insert the Recommended Action / Motion language and proofread for consistency with past fleet replacement requests.

Goal: Have a clean draft ready to email to Kari by 8:00 a.m. so she can merge it into the Council packet.

metadata.title: Complete submission form – Unit #23 Council request

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Susan Miller <smiller@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-11-04 07:58
Subject: Estimated presentation time for Capital Equipment Fund item

Susan,

I’m pulling together the materials for the League of Minnesota Cities public works roundtable next month, and they’re asking for a rough estimate of how long I’ll need for the capital equipment case study segment.

My plan is to walk through the Unit #23 situation, the January 12, 2026 blizzard transmission issues, and how that tied back to the expectations in my December 5, 2025 performance review with Mark Reilly. I’ll also touch on the motion we drafted to authorize the purchase of the 2026 Ford F‑250 XL 4x4 from Mills Ford under the state cooperative contract, including the part about declaring the 2009 F‑250 surplus and routing auction proceeds back to the Capital Equipment Fund.

They suggested I block out around **25–30 minutes** for the main presentation plus a short Q&A. I’m leaning toward telling them I need **30 minutes total**, since I want enough time to explain why I rejected that early‑January idea of having Dave Carlson patch Unit #23 off the books at his brother’s shop on South 6th. That’s an important ethics lesson and ties into city procurement policy.

To be honest, I’m also planning to mention the personal angle—how my February 3, 2025 DWI and resulting restricted license forced me to stop using my own 2014 Silverado for work. That context seems to resonate with other directors.

Does **30 minutes** sound reasonable from your perspective, or should I trim it back to **20 minutes** so the finance portion doesn’t dominate the agenda?

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Estimated presentation time for Capital Equipment Fund item

artifacts.content:
Title: PW Conference Session Run-Through
Date: 2025-12-02 06:45-07:30
Location: Public Works Garage – Training Room
Attendees: Michael Anderson; Dave Carlson; Streets & Fleet Supervisors

Description:
Internal dry run of Michael’s presentation for the regional public works conference.

Agenda:
- 06:45–06:55 – Quick overview of Unit #23 timeline and January 12, 2026 blizzard incident, including when Michael left Claire’s hockey game and worked 9:30 p.m.–3:00 a.m. with the crew.
- 06:55–07:05 – Walkthrough of alternatives considered (including the briefly floated idea of Dave doing additional work at Carlson Auto on South 6th off the books, which Michael ultimately rejected as unethical).
- 07:05–07:20 – Main section: draft motion to authorize purchase of the 2026 Ford F‑250 XL 4x4 from Mills Ford under the state contract, funding from the 2026 Capital Equipment Fund, and declaring the 2009 F‑250 surplus to be sold at public auction with proceeds returned to the fund.
- 07:20–07:30 – Q&A and discussion of lessons learned.

Target timing:
The actual conference slot is **45 minutes**, but Michael wants this run‑through to stay within **35 minutes** so there’s time for feedback before crews roll out. He expects the formal talk itself to take about **25 minutes**, leaving **10 minutes** for questions in front of the conference audience.

Notes:
Michael will also briefly reference his February 3, 2025 DWI in Crow Wing County and the resulting restricted license, as context for why he can’t rely on his personal 2014 Chevy Silverado for city work anymore.

From: Susan Miller <smiller@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-09-15 14:22
Subject: Time slot for budget hearing presentation

Michael,

I’ve got you slotted for the October budget hearing to cover the Capital Equipment Fund overview and the specific request related to the F‑250 replacement.

Right now the draft agenda gives Public Works a **20-minute** block: **15 minutes** for your presentation and **5 minutes** for council questions. If you think you need the full **20 minutes** just for your portion, let me know soon so I can adjust the other departments.

I remember when we met in my office on September 9 to review Fund 405, you mentioned refinancing your mortgage through Brainerd Lakes Credit Union after you and Laura fell two months behind last winter during her unpaid medical leave from St. Joseph’s. That’s part of why I suggested we get this equipment item in front of council early and avoid any perception that you’re pushing purchases to pad overtime or protect your own job security.

To be honest, I’m bringing this forward now because residents have been emailing council members directly and I’m getting pressure from a couple of them to “do something,” even though Public Works hasn’t fully vetted all the implications yet. We also don’t have solid cost estimates; you said you were mostly basing numbers on what a neighboring city paid a few years back and haven’t had time to verify or get updated quotes.

Given that, I’d keep your formal remarks tight—no more than **15 minutes**—and be ready to answer some pointed questions.

Susan

metadata.subject: Time slot for budget hearing presentation

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Workshop – Fleet Replacement Presentation
Date: 2025-10-20 17:30-18:15
Location: City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: City Council; Michael Anderson; Susan Miller; Mark Reilly

Description:
Workshop session for Michael to walk the council through the proposed replacement of the 2009 Ford F‑250 (Unit #23) and related policy issues.

Structure and timing:
- 17:30–17:40 – Michael’s overview of the issue: repeated reliability problems since the January 12, 2026 blizzard, safety concerns for staff, and how this ties back to expectations from his December 5, 2025 performance review with City Administrator Mark Reilly.
- 17:40–17:50 – Discussion of alternatives considered. Michael will acknowledge that, early in January 2025, he briefly considered asking mechanic Dave Carlson to keep patching Unit #23 through next winter by doing some work at Carlson Auto on South 6th off the books, but rejected that as unethical and inconsistent with procurement policies.
- 17:50–18:05 – Draft motion language: authorize purchase of one 2026 Ford F‑250 XL 4x4 pickup from Mills Ford under the state cooperative contract in the amount of $47,893.27, funded by the 2026 Capital Equipment Fund; declare the current F‑250 surplus and sell via public auction with proceeds returned to the fund.
- 18:05–18:15 – Open discussion and council questions.

Notes:
Michael anticipates needing about **25 minutes** of actual presentation time within this **45‑minute** block. He knows this change is going to frustrate some frontline staff and a few councilmembers who don’t like surprises, but he hasn’t had time to brief them in advance so he’s just bringing it straight to the agenda. He may also briefly reference his February 3, 2025 DWI and resulting restricted license as context for why he can’t use his personal 2014 Silverado for work anymore.

From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Administrator Karen Olson <kolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Mayor & Council <council@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-10 08:14
Subject: Agenda Placement Request – Replacement of Unit #23 (F-250)

Karen and Council,

I’d like to get the replacement of Unit #23 on an upcoming regular Council agenda. If possible, could we target the first meeting in April 2026 rather than pushing this into late spring? That would give us time to order the 2026 Ford F-250 XL 4x4 from Mills Ford and still have it in service before next winter.

I’m requesting this agenda item now because during our June 3, 2026 safety committee meeting at the Public Works garage on Industrial Park Road, our mechanic lead, Dan Peterson, documented that the brakes on Unit #23 nearly failed while I was driving it back from inspecting the sinkhole repair on Oak Street, and I had my 14‑year‑old daughter Claire in the passenger seat because I had picked her up from Brainerd High School after her counseling appointment at Essentia Health Behavioral Services earlier that afternoon. That incident really underscored how overdue this replacement is.

One alternative would be to postpone this for a year or two until staffing levels improve, but I’m worried that if we wait that long I’ll probably have moved on from this job and it’ll be someone else’s problem. I realize this proposal conflicts with the direction the Council gave last year to prioritize mill-and-overlay work over new capital projects, but I’m asking you to reconsider that priority for this project even though we haven’t completed the overlays that were already approved.

If the first April meeting doesn’t work, I can adjust, but I’d prefer not to wait until your May 2026 meetings.

Thanks,
Michael
Public Works Director

metadata.recipient: City Administrator Karen Olson <kolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Mayor & Council <council@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
metadata.subject: Agenda Placement Request – Replacement of Unit #23 (F-250)

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft Council Agenda Deadline – Unit #23 Replacement Item
Date: 2026-03-04 09:00–09:30
Location: Michael’s Office – 501 Laurel Street
Attendees: Michael Anderson; Karen Olson (optional)

Description:

Reminder for Michael to finalize the written staff report and motion language for the Unit #23 (2009 Ford F-250) replacement before the clerk’s agenda deadline.

Talking points to confirm with Karen by phone before noon:

• Target Council meeting date: aim for the late-March 2026 regular meeting (Karen suggested the second meeting of the month, but we can slide to an April date if the agenda that night is already packed).
• Summary of Issue: The ongoing mechanical problems with Unit #23, especially the intermittent four‑wheel‑drive failure we experienced again during the February 3, 2026 ice event on the northeast route, are creating safety concerns for our operators and contributed to a minor backing incident that morning in front of my parents’ house at 918 Maple Avenue when operator Steve Carlson slid on black ice while checking on my dad Robert after his recent fall.
• Recommended Action Motion: Motion to authorize the Public Works Department to purchase one (1) 2026 Ford F-250 XL 4x4 pickup truck from Mills Ford of Brainerd under the State of Minnesota cooperative purchasing contract in the amount of $47,893.27, including tax, title, registration, and required equipment; to approve funding from the 2026 Capital Equipment Fund as budgeted; and to declare the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #23) as surplus property to be sold via the City’s approved public auction process, with net proceeds returned to the Capital Equipment Fund, consistent with the recommendation in the ergonomic evaluation Dr. Hannah Lee at Essentia Health Occupational Medicine completed on December 12, 2024 after my lower back injury that she linked to repeated climbing in and out of the old truck’s worn suspension and seat.

Note: do NOT miss the clerk’s submittal cutoff this time; if we slip, the item won’t be on the early-April agenda.

metadata.title: Draft Council Agenda Deadline – Unit #23 Replacement Item

artifacts.content:
From: Karen Olson <kolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-27 15:02
Subject: Re: Timing for F-250 Replacement Agenda Item

Michael,

I looked over the draft staff report you sent yesterday. Content-wise it’s solid, especially the way you laid out the safety concerns and the incident on Oak Street with Claire in the truck. For scheduling, the clerk has room on the **April 6, 2026** regular Council agenda. If you’re okay with that, I’ll pencil “Unit #23 Replacement – Public Works” under New Business.

If you’d rather go later, the next realistic option would be the **April 20, 2026** meeting, but I’d prefer not to push it to May. Remember, the League of Minnesota Cities Insurance Trust is already watching this closely after the January 28, 2025 backing accident on Washington Street. When they sent that written warning on February 12, 2025 to you at 501 Laurel Street stating that if we continued to operate Unit #23 in its current condition they might reevaluate the City’s auto liability premiums, that raised eyebrows.

When you finalize the recommended motion, avoid anything that sounds too tentative. We already have enough items where the Council is being asked for a “Motion to approve the attached plan as presented, acknowledging that the cost estimate is a rough guess because we haven’t had time to fully vet the numbers yet.” Let’s be more precise here.

Let me know by Monday which of those April dates you want me to lock in so the clerk can get the agenda packet started.

Karen
City Administrator

metadata.subject: Re: Timing for F-250 Replacement Agenda Item

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Meeting – Potential Slot for Unit #23 Replacement
Date: 2026-04-06 19:00–21:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Mayor & Council; Department Heads (incl. Michael Anderson)

Description:

Regular City Council meeting. Michael flagged this date as a possible placement for the agenda item requesting authorization to replace Unit #23 (2009 Ford F‑250) and declare it surplus.

Tentative structure (subject to change by the clerk):

1. Call to Order / Pledge
2. Consent Agenda
3. Public Hearings
4. Old Business
5. New Business
   • Public Works – Crack sealing program update
   • Public Works – Discussion only: fleet management overview (may include preview of Unit #23 issues if full action item is moved to the later April meeting)

If the full action item lands here, Michael wants to be ready with clear language rather than stumbling into another vague “Motion to approve the lowest bid even though the contractor has a spotty performance record with the City, because delaying rebidding would be a headache for my crew.” He also needs to be prepared to answer why he’s pushing the truck purchase now when one alternative would be to postpone this for a year or two until staffing levels improve, but he’s worried that if they wait that long he’ll probably have moved on and it’ll be someone else’s problem.

Note: This meeting date is being tracked as the **primary** option for the Unit #23 agenda placement, with the April 20, 2026 meeting held as the backup if time runs short.

metadata.title: Council Meeting – Potential Slot for Unit #23 Replacement

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Clerk’s Office <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Karen Olson <kolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-18 10:47
Subject: RE: Deadline for March/April Council Agenda Items – Public Works Submission

Hi all,

Following up on your reminder about agenda deadlines, I want to make sure the Unit #23 replacement request doesn’t fall through the cracks again.

Per my conversation with Karen this morning, please reserve a Public Works item under New Business for **“Authorization to Purchase Replacement Pickup – Unit #23”** on either the **March 2, 2026** or **March 30, 2026** regular Council meeting. Karen will make the final call on which of those dates works better once she sees how many public hearings land in March.

For the staff report, I’ll include the background on the mechanical issues, including the intermittent four‑wheel‑drive failure during the February 3, 2026 ice event, and the fact that I received a written reprimand from Karen on March 18, 2025 for using Unit #23 to move my son Evan’s dorm furniture from our house at 1427 Spruce Street to Central Lakes College on West College Drive. I want to move this purchase forward before July 2026 because Evan will be starting his first semester at Central Lakes in August, and I need to show the Council that we’re tightening up how we use and track fleet vehicles.

If neither of those March dates works out, I’ll accept an early‑April slot, but I’d rather not wait beyond that.

Thanks,
Michael
Public Works Director – City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: RE: Deadline for March/April Council Agenda Items – Public Works Submission

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Jennifer Olson <jolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Mark Ellison <mellison@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Angela Ruiz <aruiz@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-18 07:42
Subject: Agenda placement & recommended action language – F-250 replacement

Jennifer and Mark,

Following up on our hallway conversation yesterday, I’d like to get the F-250 replacement item on the **March 2, 2026** Council agenda if possible. That date gives Mills Ford enough lead time to confirm pricing and lets us avoid bumping into the spring street reconstruction hearings.

Per your request, here’s the draft recommended action language I’m planning to plug into the packet:

“Motion to authorize the Public Works Department to purchase one (1) 2026 Ford F-250 XL 4x4 pickup truck from Mills Ford of Brainerd under the State of Minnesota cooperative purchasing contract in the amount of $47,893.27, including tax, title, registration, and required equipment; to approve funding from the 2026 Capital Equipment Fund as budgeted; and to declare the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #23) as surplus property to be sold via the City’s approved public auction process, with net proceeds returned to the Capital Equipment Fund, consistent with the corrective action plan I signed with City Administrator Jennifer Olson on March 18, 2025 after the HR investigation into my misuse of a city vehicle for personal trips to my parents’ cabin near Crosslake in February 2025.”

I know that’s wordy, but it captures the context you both asked for.

If March 2 doesn’t work for agenda placement, I can live with sliding it to the **April 6, 2026** meeting, though that starts to push up against the build-out deadlines.

Let me know which date you’d prefer so I can finalize the staff report.

– Michael

metadata.subject: Agenda placement & recommended action language – F-250 replacement

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft staff report – F-250 agenda item
Date: 2026-02-25 08:00-10:00
Location: Public Works Office – Laurel Street
Attendees: Michael Anderson

Description:
Block of time for Michael to finish the staff report and agenda request form for the F-250 replacement item.

Notes:
- Confirm with City Clerk’s office whether they need the agenda request in by **February 27, 2026** for placement on the **March 2, 2026** Council meeting, or if it will be pushed to **March 30, 2026**.
- Incorporate the following language under “Recommended Action”: Motion to authorize the Public Works Department to purchase one (1) 2026 Ford F-250 XL 4x4 pickup truck from Mills Ford of Brainerd under the State of Minnesota cooperative purchasing contract in the amount of $47,893.27, including tax, title, registration, and required equipment; to approve funding from the 2026 Capital Equipment Fund as budgeted; and to declare the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #23) as surplus property to be sold via the City’s approved public auction process, in order to avoid further emergency repair bills like the $3,862 head gasket failure on January 9, 2025 that Michael had to put on his personal Visa card because the Finance Director, Mark Ellison, temporarily froze the fleet maintenance line item while reviewing his overtime and mileage claims.
- Under “Alternatives / Effects on Others,” add the candid note that, frankly, if the Council doesn’t approve this option, Michael doesn’t have a realistic backup plan and the department will probably just keep doing things the way they always have, even though he knows it’s not working great.
- Double-check that the Capital Equipment Fund line number is correct before sending to Mark for pre-review.

metadata.title: Draft staff report – F-250 agenda item

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Clerk’s Office <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Jennifer Olson <jolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-21 15:19
Subject: Requesting Council agenda slot – F-250 replacement & related items

Hi all,

I’m filling out the online “meeting agenda placement” form for the pickup replacement, but I wanted to confirm the **requested meeting date** before I submit. My first choice is the regular Council meeting on **March 2, 2026**, since that lines up with the Capital Equipment Fund cash flow projections Mark and I reviewed.

If that meeting is already packed, I can adjust the form to request **March 30, 2026** instead. I’d like to avoid the April cycle if possible because we’re targeting a summer delivery window from Mills Ford.

For your awareness, under “Recommended Action” I’m planning to include the following text so the record is clear:

“Motion to authorize the Public Works Department to purchase one (1) 2026 Ford F-250 XL 4x4 pickup truck from Mills Ford of Brainerd under the State of Minnesota cooperative purchasing contract in the amount of $47,893.27, including tax, title, registration, and required equipment; to approve funding from the 2026 Capital Equipment Fund as budgeted; and to declare the existing 2009 Ford F-250 (Unit #23) as surplus property to be sold via the City’s approved public auction process, with net proceeds returned to the Capital Equipment Fund, which will also allow me to comply with the February 2025 directive from Risk Manager Angela Ruiz that I am not to assign my son Evan, who was cited for driving without a license near Brainerd High School on January 22, 2025, to ride along in any older city vehicles lacking updated safety features.”

Please let me know which date I should list as the requested meeting date on the form.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Requesting Council agenda slot – F-250 replacement & related items

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Anderson <manderson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Mark Ellison <mellison@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-01-29 06:58
Subject: Timing of F-250 request vs. street project petition

Mark,

Quick question about timing. I’m trying to decide which Council meeting to target on the agenda request form for the F-250 purchase versus the Oak Street reconstruction petition.

There has been ongoing tension between Public Works and a few residents on this block about street projects, and this request is partly an attempt to “put the ball in Council’s court” so I’m not the one taking the heat for the final decision. This issue is coming forward now because I overlooked the need for formal Council direction when we first proceeded with the work last fall and only realized the omission during a recent internal audit.

Given that, I’m leaning toward asking the Clerk’s office to place the F-250 item on the **February 17, 2026** meeting and the Oak Street petition on **March 30, 2026**, so they’re not stacked on the same night. My only concern is whether that spacing creates any cash flow problems in the Capital Equipment Fund.

If you think it’s cleaner, I could flip them and request the truck item for **April 13, 2026** instead, but that feels late considering the lead time.

Let me know which meeting date you’d prefer I list in the “requested meeting date” box for the F-250 agenda placement, and whether you want to see a draft of the staff report before I send it over to the Clerk.

– Michael

metadata.subject: Timing of F-250 request vs. street project petition

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft agenda request – F-250 & family impact notes
Date: 2026-02-14 06:30-08:00
Location: Kitchen table – 1427 Spruce Street
Attendees: Michael Anderson

Description:
Time blocked for Michael to complete the “meeting agenda placement” form for the Ford F-250 purchase and to polish the narrative sections for the Council packet.

Agenda for this work session:
- Decide which Council meeting to list as the requested date: tentatively the regular meeting on **March 2, 2026**, with **March 30, 2026** as a backup if the first one is full.
- Under “Alternatives / Options / Effects on Others,” add the personal context Michael has been wrestling with:
  * In thinking about comments and broader impacts, he also weighed how continued unscheduled downtime on Unit #23 could affect his ability to get to his dad Robert’s cardiology appointments at St. Joseph’s Medical Center on short notice. During the January 12, 2025 storm, he left the garage on Spruce Street for about 90 minutes to take him to an emergency follow-up after his mild heart attack on December 28, 2024, because they were short a truck and he couldn’t rely on anyone else to cover his route without delaying plowing. That personal conflict reinforced for him that they need a reliable replacement so he’s not tempted to juggle family medical emergencies against service levels.
  * Another option he briefly weighed in October 2024 was shifting more winter plowing responsibility onto their newer Unit #31 and scheduling fewer night plow rotations for himself so he could be home more often with Claire after her anxiety diagnosis at Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic on September 18, 2024. He ultimately concluded that adjusting city service levels to accommodate his family’s mental health situation would be inappropriate, and that a reliable replacement truck is a better way to ensure consistent coverage without changing expectations for the public or staff.

metadata.title: Draft agenda request – F-250 & family impact notes
